name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
description: A retirement-maximising portfolio built specifically for a Switzerland-based investor, optimising for long-run real CHF purchasing power. The core holds broad global equity with a deliberate factor satellite targeting the small-cap value premium (AVUV, AVDV). Global listed real estate (VNQI) and physical gold (IAU) provide inflation resilience, while a modest bond sleeve seeds the future retirement glidepath. Note: original portfolio used Swiss-domiciled ETFs (VWRL, CHSPI, CSGOLD etc.) — replaced here with closest US-listed equivalents for tracking purposes.

name: ChatGPT 5.4
description: A Swiss retirement-focused, global core-satellite portfolio built to maximize long-run real wealth. Uses global equities as the main growth engine, adds small-cap value and quality tilts for higher expected long-term return, and includes a small allocation to bonds and gold for stability and inflation resilience. Note: original portfolio used Swiss-domiciled ETFs (CHSPI, SGLN, CSBGC3, CHCORP) — replaced here with closest US-listed equivalents for tracking purposes.

name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
description: A Swiss-Anchored Global Factor Barbell strategy designed to maximize long-term real wealth. Balances core global equity beta (VOO, VEA) and US tech momentum (QQQM) with small-cap value factors (AVUV, AVDV). An emerging markets sleeve (AVEM) captures demographic growth. Note: original portfolio included CHSPI for Swiss home bias — replaced with VEA as closest liquid US-listed equivalent.

name: DeepSeek R1
description: Targets 5.5-6.5% annualized returns over 40 years by combining global equity beta with factor tilts (small-cap value via AVUV/AVDV), and a gold hedge (IAU) against inflation. Minimal bonds (BND) provide future glidepath ballast. Note: original portfolio used Swiss-domiciled ETFs (CHSPI, PGHN, IWDA, ZPRV, ZPRX, CHBAA) — replaced here with closest US-listed equivalents for tracking purposes.

name: Perplexity
description: A global, equity-dominant retirement portfolio with tilts to global small caps, emerging markets, and quality, complemented by a small allocation to global REITs, gold, and bonds for regime diversification. Note: original portfolio used Swiss/European ETFs (VWRL, CHSPI, WSML, VFEM) — replaced here with closest US-listed equivalents for tracking purposes.
